scope_note: QA Generator-Pattern Training Cert family [258]: certifies that training in a QA-native architecture is discrete-search identification of an integer generator pattern, not stochastic gradient descent on float weights. The trained object is an integer tuple (b_0, e_0) in {1..m}^2 (generator starting state) whose T-orbit matches a target trace by exact tuple-equality count. No gradients, no float optimizer state (no Adam m/v), no importance-sampling corrections, no staleness bounds. Structurally eliminates the GLM-5 async-RL policy-staleness failure mode (arXiv:2602.15763, §4.1.2): there is no float policy to drift, so staleness/IS/optimizer-reset are all inapplicable. Evaluation is orbit-trace match (integer-valued), not scalar loss. T1 path-time is native: training steps are discrete hops in generator space. Connects [209] Signal Generator Inference (e_t = ((b_{t+1}-b_t-1)%m)+1 — the canonical integer generator), [256] QA Orbit-Resonance Attention (uses this training signature), [257] QA Integer-State Pipeline (training loop stays inside the two-boundary invariant). Source: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-19. Reference prototype: qa_lab/qa_orbit_resonance_attention.py (identify_generator, identify_family). Design doc: docs/theory/QA_GLM5_ARCHITECTURE_MAPPING.md §Failure-Mode-3. Checks GPT_1+INT_GEN/NO_GRAD/DISCRETE/ORBIT_EVAL/DET/SRC/WIT/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
